title: Fear Garden — Level 2 Rookie Detective
description: LEVEL 2 ROOKIE DETECTIVE CASE version of Fear Garden with unique suspect, method, motive, evidence, and answers.

answerKey.method: Swapped Nolan’s marked beer flight with a tainted sample during the garden toast
answerKey.motive: To hide that Trevor had been watering down premium kegs and pocketing the difference
answerKey.keyEvidence: Tap-room pour log, flight marker photo, and deleted keg inventory note
answerKey.explanation: Trevor Knox had the access, timing, and motive. The proof path combines tap-room pour log, flight marker photo, and deleted keg inventory note with the failed statement: Trevor claimed he stayed at the patio gate.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Fear Garden begins at Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. This session is Level 2: Rookie Detective Case.

publicClues.title: Difficulty Loaded
publicClues.text: This is Level 2: Rookie Detective Case. Evidence and answers are unique to this level.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Answer the checkpoint after reviewing the body scene.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Compare the timeline before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Digital and venue systems now matter.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Find the motive evidence.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all 10 total questions before the lock.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Nolan Graves missed two calls from Trevor Knox before the garden music cut out.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final call lasted eleven seconds as Nolan moved toward the side corridor.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Nolan texted Trevor: “We talk tonight. I saw the numbers.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Trevor replied: “Not at the bar. Meet by the garden gate.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Nolan’s phone pinged near the patio garden path at 8:07 PM.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Trevor’s device appears nearby during the same short window.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A Beer Garden event ledger shows a suspicious private payout connected to hide that trevor had been watering down premium kegs and pocketing the difference.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payout trail links to a device or account controlled by Trevor.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo shows Trevor near the restricted service path before the incident.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another photo captures the scene detail: Nolan’s marked flight glass changes trays.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A patio livestream catches a missing gap just before Nolan disappears from frame.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The posted version removes the seconds that would show who crossed behind the garden wall.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Trevor saved a contact under “Garden Services,” matching a suspicious payment line.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Nolan saved the same number with the note “ask about fake paperwork.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the killer needed venue access, timing, and a reason to silence Nolan.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the scene points to planning, not a random Beer Garden accident.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: A security export skips the exact Beer Garden patio window.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: File metadata connects the edit to the suspect’s access trail: Trevor Knox.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A browser search asks how to remove timestamp metadata from venue clips.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search matches the method: swapped nolan’s marked beer flight with a tainted sample during the garden toast.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Fear Garden at Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. You are playing Level 2: Rookie Detective Case. Study each app carefully. Every level has its own truth path, evidence trail, and final answer set.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 2: Rookie Detective Case. The culprit was Trevor Knox. The method: Swapped Nolan’s marked beer flight with a tainted sample during the garden toast. The motive: To hide that Trevor had been watering down premium kegs and pocketing the difference. The proof: Tap-room pour log, flight marker photo, and deleted keg inventory note.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What makes Nolan’s death suspicious?

options.label: A normal spilled pint near the stage
A table number written backward
Nolan’s marked flight glass changes trays
A guest closing a patio umbrella

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which timeline clue matters most?

options.label: The DJ restarted a playlist
A guest paid with cash
A server dropped a tray outside
Trevor’s pour code appears during the toast window

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which digital clue suggests planning?

options.label: premium keg yield is impossibly high
A low battery warning
A misspelled hashtag
A normal Wi-Fi reconnect

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 4: What clue points toward motive?

options.label: A birthday receipt
a deleted inventory note names Trevor’s side cash
A lost wristband
An ordinary tab transfer

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: Which statement begins to collapse?

options.label: Maya said the plants were watered
Janelle said the gate was busy
Trevor claimed he stayed at the patio gate
Darius said the speaker was loud
Maya Bell
Janelle Price
Darius Cole
Trevor Knox

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Swapped Nolan’s marked beer flight with a tainted sample during the garden toast
Poisoned a shared punch bowl
Cut the stage lights only
Staged a random parking-lot fall
To win a trivia prize
To hide that Trevor had been watering down premium kegs and pocketing the difference
To protect a karaoke score
To hide a family recipe

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves it?

options.label: A guest rumor and a napkin
A broken patio chair only
Tap-room pour log, flight marker photo, and deleted keg inventory note
A single missed call with no context

questions.prompt: Final 5: Which suspect statement falls apart?

options.label: Maya said the music was loud
Trevor said the patio was crowded
Janelle said the menu was changed
Trevor claimed he stayed at the patio gate
